title: Wreck: three broken rules
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the page instead of fixing it. Bring the code back.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was. All three broken things are still there.
hint2.untouched: Look at the preview. None of the four rules works. One at a time.
why.not-closed: An HTML tag is open and never closed.
hint2.not-closed: Check <style> and </style>. What's broken is the CSS, not the HTML — leave the other tags alone.
why.no-h1: There is no heading. You deleted the <h1> instead of fixing the rule for it.
hint2.no-h1: You're fixing styles. The HTML below is fine and stays untouched.
why.no-p: There is not a single paragraph left. You deleted them.
hint2.no-p: The two lines of text are fine. What's broken is above, between <style> and </style>.
why.no-background: The background isn't cream. The body rule is there, it looks fine — and it doesn't work.
hint2.no-background: Look at the end of the background-color line. Compare it with the end of the line below.
hint3.no-background: A semicolon isn't decoration. It says "this declaration is done". Without it the browser reads the next line as a continuation of the same value — and throws both away. That's why the font doesn't work either.
why.no-font: The font isn't Georgia. That rule is written correctly — it dies with the line above it.
hint2.no-font: This line isn't broken on its own. The one before it is.
hint3.no-font: The browser doesn't stop on an error. It quietly discards what it can't parse and moves on. That's how one mistake kills two lines while you only see the result.
hint4.no-font: Fix the line above and this one fixes itself.
why.h1-not-red: The heading isn't dark red. The rule is exact, the colour exists — and still nothing.
hint2.h1-not-red: Count the curly braces on the h1 rule. Opening and closing.
hint3.h1-not-red: Without a closing brace the browser thinks the rule continues. The next rule becomes part of it — and is lost along with it.
hint4.h1-not-red: Close the h1 rule before the p rule starts.
why.p-not-brown: The paragraphs aren't brown. This one is a different mistake — not punctuation, but the value itself.
hint2.p-not-brown: Read what comes after color. Is that a colour?
hint3.p-not-brown: Every property accepts a certain kind of value. color wants a colour. font-size wants a size. Give a size to a colour and the browser throws the whole line away — silently, without a word.
hint4.p-not-brown: Change the value to a colour. The paragraphs should be saddlebrown.
why.lost-p: A paragraph is gone. You're fixing styles, not deleting text.
blocks.0.text: So far you've seen what a rule looks like. Now you'll see what a BROKEN rule looks like — and why that's the more useful skill.
blocks.3.text: Selector — which elements. Curly braces — how far it reaches. Inside: property, colon, value, semicolon. Four parts, and every one of them breaks in its own way.
blocks.5.text: Here's the important part of this lesson. The browser does NOT stop on a CSS error. No red, no message, no line number. It discards what it can't understand and carries on as if nothing happened.
blocks.6.items.0: Missing semicolon — you lose that line AND the next one.
blocks.6.items.1: Missing curly brace — you lose the entire following rule.
blocks.6.items.2: Wrong kind of value — you lose just that line, quietly.
blocks.7.text: The silence isn't malicious. It's deliberate: a page with one broken character should still render rather than go black. You're the one who pays for it, while you hunt.
blocks.9.text: There are four rules on the right and not one of them works. Three different broken things are keeping them dead. Fix them.
blocks.10.text: The background should be cornsilk, the font Georgia, the heading darkred, the paragraphs saddlebrown. Nobody will tell you where the mistake is — exactly like tomorrow, in real work.
